The '''Jeby''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] that dwelled on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Rainboh]], a planet of the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]]. They were jovial creatures, shedding their pink or red spots whenever they laughed. The Jeby then had to use their twelve [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] to gather these spots and reattach them to their bodies. Jeby had three long necks, which terminated in three round heads, each with a single hair at its apex. They stood on four thin legs, which were striped white and red.

==Biology and appearance==
Each Jeby had three heads and a scaly, [[Color/Legends|white]] body covered in pink or red spots. Located at the lateral sides of the body were six [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]], for a total of twelve. Individuals stood on four legs. A Jeby's spots had a tendency to detach when an individual laughed, but they could be sucked back up by the Jeby's tentacles. These manipulators could also be used to handle other objects. Although the tentacles were primarily white, each featured a pink or red band at the midpoint and the tip. The Jeby's legs, while also white, were banded pink and red.<ref name="daa" />

The three Jeby necks originated from a single point on the body and were segmented into two different colors at their midpoints—for example, one neck could be red and yellow, while another was green and light green. The Jeby face featured two black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a nose, and a mouth. Atop each of these circular heads, a Jeby sported a single black hair, which stood straight up.<ref name="daa" />

==Behavior==
Jeby were prone to mirth—their laughter caused the pink spots covering their body to fall off. As a result, a Jeby had to recollect their lost body parts with their tentacles and restore them to the body. When detached, the spots of the Jeby littered the surface of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Rainboh]].<ref name="daa" />

==History==
The Jeby dwelled on the rainbow-colored [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of Rainboh,<ref name="daa" /> which was located within the [[Hapan Rim Worlds]] of the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]]. This area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] was presumed to have been constructed by a group of beings known as the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]]. Although the cluster was reached by outsiders between [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000]] and [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], it remained cut off from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large from [[3100 BBY]] until [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Seven-year-old [[Paul Rice]] created the Jeby through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition held in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine, which asked ''[[Star Wars]]'' fans to create completely new [[Alien/Legends|alien]] creatures inspired by the [[Original trilogy|original]] ''Star Wars'' trilogy. The contest was initiated in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]], which inspired Rice's first-grade teacher at a [[Wikipedia:Cleveland Heights, Ohio|Cleveland Heights]] primary school to use it as an art project for the students. ''Star Wars Galaxy'' received several entries from the class, but Rice's was the only one published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]). The Jeby came under the "honorable mentions" section of the "under 16" subdivision. Rice's description of the Jeby leaves the beings' [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]] ambiguous.<ref name="daa" />

''Star Wars'' continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] has stated that published "Design an Alien" material is considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> The planet Rainboh, which was created in Rice's entry, is included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]), by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]].
